Field Guide Print Summary

Field Reference Guide

Standard Operational Definitions & Classifications (PAGASA / DOST)

Typhoon Classifications

Category Wind Speed (kph) Description & Action
Tropical Depression (TD) Up to 61 Twigs of small trees may be broken. Rice and corn may be adversely affected. Action: Monitor updates.
Tropical Storm (TS) 62 - 88 Few big trees may be uprooted. Light to moderate damage to shanties. Action: Secure outdoor items.
Severe Tropical Storm (STS) 89 - 117 Medium to heavy damage to agriculture. Some old galvanized iron roofs may be peeled off. Action: Prepare for possible evacuation.
Typhoon (TY) 118 - 184 Heavy damage to high-risk structures. Vast damage to agriculture. Widespread power disruption. Action: Evacuate low-lying areas.
Super Typhoon (STY) 185+ Widespread and very heavy damage. Most residential and institutional buildings may be severely damaged. Action: SEEK SHELTER IMMEDIATELY.

Monsoons

Amihan (NE)

Cool, dry. Oct-Mar.

Habagat (SW)

Warm, moist. May-Sep.

Weather Icons

Sunny/Clear
Cloudy
Rain
Thunderstorm

Weather Systems & Terms

PAR

Philippine Area of Responsibility

ITCZ

Intertropical Convergence Zone. A band of thunderstorms where trade winds from both hemispheres meet near the equator. Brings scattered to heavy rains and thunderstorms.

Shear Line

A boundary between wind masses with different speeds/directions. Causes localized rain, thunderstorms, and moderate wind.

Habagat

Southwest Monsoon. Brings heavy rainfall and strong winds to western areas (May-Sept). Often intensifies rainfall events.

Amihan

Northeast Monsoon. Brings cooler, drier weather to eastern areas (Nov-April). Generally brings fair skies.

LPA

Low Pressure Area. Below-normal atmospheric pressure. Brings clouds, rain, and potential cyclone development.

HPA

High Pressure Area. Above-normal atmospheric pressure. Brings fair, clear skies and good visibility.

TD

Tropical Depression. Organized cyclone with max winds <63 km/h. Close to tropical storm development.

TS

Tropical Storm. Organized cyclone with max winds 63-118 km/h. Dangerous winds and heavy rainfall.

PAGASA

Philippine Atmospheric, Geophysical & Astronomical Services Admin

Rainfall Warning

Yellow (Monitor)

7.5-15mm/hr

Orange (Alert)

15-30mm/hr

Red (Emergency)

>30mm/hr

Heat Index

Caution

27°C - 32°C

Extreme

33°C - 41°C

Danger

42°C+

Pressure Areas

LPA

Low Pressure Area. Brings clouds, rain, and bad weather.

HPA

High Pressure Area. Brings fair, clear skies.
